search_queries_used: site:nice.org.uk NG19 diabetic foot examine both feet neuropathy ischaemia ulcer callus infection
candidate_sources_rejected: automatic diabetic-foot risk category or active-problem diagnosis
automatic referral, debridement, dressing, offloading, antibiotic, imaging, or follow-up interval
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: Risk classification requires complete bilateral assessment and verified history.
No management or referral was generated.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: Adults and age-appropriate young people with diabetes; age-specific recommendations apply.
setting_applicability: Primary, diabetes, podiatry, and multidisciplinary foot assessment.
UAE_applicability: NICE evidence requires UAE diabetes, podiatry, vascular, wound, infection, emergency, referral, and facility adaptation.
recency_verification: Current NICE NG19 recommendations, including displayed 2023 amendments, were reviewed on 2026-07-14.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: NG19 directly supports clinician examination of both feet for skin integrity, callus, ulceration, infection or inflammation, deformity, neuropathy, perfusion, gangrene, and Charcot features, with clinician-entered risk classification only.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports documenting age-appropriate review, bilateral skin and sensory examination, perfusion, ulceration, callus, infection or inflammation, deformity, gangrene, Charcot features, and clinician-entered risk classification without calculating it.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The foot-service section supports recording a completed specialist assessment, information already provided, clinician-selected review plan, and arranged follow-up without generating referral or an interval.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports documenting completed foot protection assessment, vascular or biomechanical review, clinician-selected reassessment, information already provided, and an entered follow-up plan.

unresolved_source_gaps: Diabetes type and control, previous ulcer or amputation, renal replacement, bilateral skin findings, pulses, neuropathy testing, footwear, ulcer dimensions, infection, Charcot features, clinician risk category, treatment, referral, and follow-up remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
